name: Lamb w/ Yoghurt, Coconut Milk & Almond Masala
recipeInstructions:
1. Drop the saffron threads into a small bowl or cup, add 1/3 of the boiling water and soak for at least 10 minutes.
2. Pour the saffron and its soaking liquid into a deep bowl and stir in the yoghurt, caraway seeds, and salt.
3. Add the lamb and turn it with a spoon until all pieces are evenly coated. Marinate the lamb at room temperature for about 30 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, combine the almonds and the rest of the boiling water in a bowl, and soak for 10 minutes. Pour the almonds and their soaking water into a blender and blend until you have a smooth paste. Set aside.
5. In a heavy casserole, heat the ghee over moderate heat until fairly hot. Add the cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves, stir for a minute or so, then add the onions, garlic, and ginger.
6. Lift and turn them constantly, frying for 7 to 8 minutes until the onions are soft and golden brown. With a slotted spoon, remove the lamb from the marinade and add the meat to the casserole.
7. Stir over moderate heat until it browns evenly. Stir in the marinade, cold water, almond puree, and red pepper, then cook for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
8. Pour in the coconut milk, bring to a boil, and simmer partially covered for 20 minutes, or until the lamb is tender. To serve, discard the cinnamon and cloves, mound the lamb attractively on a deep heated platter, and pour the sauce over it.
